alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window at critical 1-2 weeks amid deal collapse risk
alerts.summary: US intelligence continues to assess Iran's uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with breakout time estimated at 1-2 weeks (global_metrics). Simultaneously, incidents feed reports Iran warns Israeli strikes on Lebanon could derail US-Iran agreement, signaling Tehran may use escalation pretext to abandon nuclear commitments. BBC and Reuters headlines confirm deal terms remain unresolved and Iran has 'continuously violated commitments.'

alerts.title: Israel continues Beirut strikes; Iran military threatens harsh response if attacks persist
alerts.summary: Multiple Israeli drone and air strikes hit southern Beirut suburbs and southern Lebanon, killing at least 7 civilians (Reuters, NBC News, incidents feed). Iran's military issued direct threat that Israel 'should expect harsh response' if southern Lebanon attacks continue (Ynetnews). Represents clear escalation trigger for US-Iran ceasefire collapse as both sides cited Lebanon as red line.

alerts.title: Trump publicly distances from Israel's Lebanon strikes amid Iran deal fragility
alerts.summary: President Trump condemned Israel's Beirut attack as 'not responsible' and urged Netanyahu to exercise restraint on Lebanon operations (Al Jazeera, Axios, incidents feed). Statement contradicts Trump's earlier vow to strike Iran 'very hard' and signals White House concern that Israeli escalation will trigger Iranian walkout from nascent nuclear agreement, jeopardizing $300B financial component.

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber group claims hack of FBI drone fleet; threatens World Cup targets
alerts.summary: Iran-linked threat actor claimed successful compromise of US FBI drone systems with stated intent to disrupt World Cup 2026, per cyber_headlines (France 24, CBS News). Separate claims of California water system hack in retaliation for prior Iran strikes. Represents expansion of Iranian cyber operations into critical US infrastructure and symbolic targets amid nuclear deal negotiations.

alerts.title: Three US carrier strike groups maintain elevated posture across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Mediterranean
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) remain deployed with supporting destroyers (USS Carney, Mason, Laboon) and amphibious assets (USS Bataan) per assets feed. Deployment reflects US force readiness against Iran proxy escalation and Israeli-Hezbollah spillover as threat level stands at 9/10 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Mediterranean

analysis_note: US-Iran nuclear ceasefire framework is on the verge of collapse due to Israeli-Lebanese escalation spillover, Iranian breakout window shrinking to weeks, and mutual accusations of bad faith compliance; simultaneously, cyber and proxy warfare intensify while Trump administration applies contradictory messaging on Israel restraint and Iranian military targets.
